source.name: Fishing Bay Marina official pages / Waterway Guide

standard.notes: Official page lists monohull nightly dockage at $2.50/ft, catamaran nightly at $4.50/ft, and a $100 nightly minimum. Weekly pricing is published separately.

cautions.text: Official transient rates include a $100 nightly minimum, which matters for smaller boats.

expert_notes.plain: One of the stronger Deltaville transient choices because it publishes real rates, has useful services, and works well as a Chesapeake stepping-stone stop. It is, however, on Fishing Bay and many pass-through cruisers consider it a longer run in than the Broad Creek / Regatta Point side.

cautions.text: Official marina materials indicate 6 to 9 foot depths, so deeper-draft boats should confirm carefully.

expert_notes.plain: A practical Deltaville transient stop with published rates, electric included, and a useful service-yard profile, but depth should be checked carefully by deeper-draft boats.

name: Regatta Point Yachting Center

standard.notes: Official site confirms 14 transient slips are always available, but current dockage pricing should be confirmed directly.

cautions.text: A strong transient and weather-wait choice, but not the place to plan a fuel stop.

expert_notes.plain: This is one of the easiest and most favored Deltaville pass-through transient choices. It is on the more convenient Broad Creek / Regatta Point side rather than the longer run into Fishing Bay. Approaching Broad Creek, watch for shoaling at WR3 and favor center or slightly right of center for the best water at low tide.
